relatedVulnerabilities.description: The NPM package `braces`, versions prior to 3.0.3, fails to limit the number of characters it can handle, which could lead to Memory Exhaustion. In `lib/parse.js,` if a malicious user sends "imbalanced braces" as input, the parsing will enter a loop, which will cause the program to start allocating heap memory without freeing it at any moment of the loop. Eventually, the JavaScript heap limit is reached, and the program will crash.

relatedVulnerabilities.description: Express.js minimalist web framework for node. Versions of Express.js prior to 4.19.0 and all pre-release alpha and beta versions of 5.0 are affected by an open redirect vulnerability using malformed URLs. When a user of Express performs a redirect using a user-provided URL Express performs an encode [using `encodeurl`](https://github.com/pillarjs/encodeurl) on the contents before passing it to the `location` header. This can cause malformed URLs to be evaluated in unexpected ways by common redirect allow list implementations in Express applications, leading to an Open Redirect via bypass of a properly implemented allow list. The main method impacted is `res.location()` but this is also called from within `res.redirect()`. The vulnerability is fixed in 4.19.2 and 5.0.0-beta.3.

vulnerability.description: Express.js Open Redirect in malformed URLs

relatedVulnerabilities.description: minimatch is a minimal matching utility for converting glob expressions into JavaScript RegExp objects. Prior to version 10.2.3, 9.0.7, 8.0.6, 7.4.8, 6.2.2, 5.1.8, 4.2.5, and 3.1.4, nested `*()` extglobs produce regexps with nested unbounded quantifiers (e.g. `(?:(?:a|b)*)*`), which exhibit catastrophic backtracking in V8. With a 12-byte pattern `*(*(*(a|b)))` and an 18-byte non-matching input, `minimatch()` stalls for over 7 seconds. Adding a single nesting level or a few input characters pushes this to minutes. This is the most severe finding: it is triggered by the default `minimatch()` API with no special options, and the minimum viable pattern is only 12 bytes. The same issue affects `+()` extglobs equally. Versions 10.2.3, 9.0.7, 8.0.6, 7.4.8, 6.2.2, 5.1.8, 4.2.5, and 3.1.4 fix the issue.

vulnerability.description: minimatch ReDoS: nested *() extglobs generate catastrophically backtracking regular expressions

relatedVulnerabilities.description: minimatch is a minimal matching utility for converting glob expressions into JavaScript RegExp objects. Prior to version 10.2.3, 9.0.7, 8.0.6, 7.4.8, 6.2.2, 5.1.8, 4.2.5, and 3.1.3, `matchOne()` performs unbounded recursive backtracking when a glob pattern contains multiple non-adjacent `**` (GLOBSTAR) segments and the input path does not match. The time complexity is O(C(n, k)) -- binomial -- where `n` is the number of path segments and `k` is the number of globstars. With k=11 and n=30, a call to the default `minimatch()` API stalls for roughly 5 seconds. With k=13, it exceeds 15 seconds. No memoization or call budget exists to bound this behavior. Any application where an attacker can influence the glob pattern passed to `minimatch()` is vulnerable. The realistic attack surface includes build tools and task runners that accept user-supplied glob arguments (ESLint, Webpack, Rollup config), multi-tenant systems where one tenant configures glob-based rules that run in a shared process, admin or developer interfaces that accept ignore-rule or filter configuration as globs, and CI/CD pipelines that evaluate user-submitted config files containing glob patterns. An attacker who can place a crafted pattern into any of these paths can stall the Node.js event loop for tens of seconds per invocation. The pattern is 56 bytes for a 5-second stall and does not require authentication in contexts where pattern input is part of the feature. Versions 10.2.3, 9.0.7, 8.0.6, 7.4.8, 6.2.2, 5.1.8, 4.2.5, and 3.1.3 fix the issue.

vulnerability.description: minimatch has ReDoS: matchOne() combinatorial backtracking via multiple non-adjacent GLOBSTAR segments

relatedVulnerabilities.description: path-to-regexp turns path strings into a regular expressions. In certain cases, path-to-regexp will output a regular expression that can be exploited to cause poor performance. Because JavaScript is single threaded and regex matching runs on the main thread, poor performance will block the event loop and lead to a DoS. The bad regular expression is generated any time you have two parameters within a single segment, separated by something that is not a period (.). For users of 0.1, upgrade to 0.1.10. All other users should upgrade to 8.0.0.

relatedVulnerabilities.description: Underscore.js is a utility-belt library for JavaScript. Prior to 1.13.8, the _.flatten and _.isEqual functions use recursion without a depth limit. Under very specific conditions, detailed below, an attacker could exploit this in a Denial of Service (DoS) attack by triggering a stack overflow. Untrusted input must be used to create a recursive datastructure, for example using JSON.parse, with no enforced depth limit. The datastructure thus created must be passed to _.flatten or _.isEqual. In the case of _.flatten, the vulnerability can only be exploited if it is possible for a remote client to prepare a datastructure that consists of arrays at all levels AND if no finite depth limit is passed as the second argument to _.flatten. In the case of _.isEqual, the vulnerability can only be exploited if there exists a code path in which two distinct datastructures that were submitted by the same remote client are compared using _.isEqual. For example, if a client submits data that are stored in a database, and the same client can later submit another datastructure that is then compared to the data that were saved in the database previously, OR if a client submits a single request, but its data are parsed twice, creating two non-identical but equivalent datastructures that are then compared. Exceptions originating from the call to _.flatten or _.isEqual, as a result of a stack overflow, are not being caught. This vulnerability is fixed in 1.13.8.

vulnerability.description: Underscore has unlimited recursion in _.flatten and _.isEqual, potential for DoS attack

configs.Resolution: Add '-y' flag to 'apt-get'
configs.Title: 'apt-get' missing '-y' to avoid manual input

configs.Description: MAINTAINER has been deprecated since Docker 1.13.0.
configs.Resolution: Use LABEL instead of MAINTAINER
configs.Title: Deprecated MAINTAINER used

configs.Description: Providing more than one HEALTHCHECK instruction per stage is confusing and error-prone.
configs.Resolution: One HEALTHCHECK instruction must remain in Dockerfile. Remove all other instructions.
configs.Title: Multiple HEALTHCHECK defined

configs.Description: You should use 'apk add' with '--no-cache' to clean package cached data and reduce image size.
configs.Resolution: Add '--no-cache' to 'apk add' in Dockerfile
configs.Title: 'apk add' is missing '--no-cache'

configs.Description: Cached package data should be cleaned after installation to reduce image size.
configs.Resolution: Add 'microdnf clean all' to Dockerfile
configs.Title: 'microdnf clean all' missing

configs.Description: WORKDIR should not be mounted on system directories to avoid container breakouts
configs.Resolution: Avoid using system directories to mount WORKDIR
configs.Title: WORKDIR should not be mounted on system dirs

configs.Description: Passing secrets via `build-args` or envs or copying secret files can leak them out
configs.Resolution: Use secret mount if secrets are needed during image build. Use volume mount if secret files are needed during container runtime.
configs.Title: Secrets passed via `build-args` or envs or copied secret files

configs.Description: You should add HEALTHCHECK instruction in your docker container images to perform the health check on running containers.
configs.Message: Add HEALTHCHECK instruction in your Dockerfile
configs.Resolution: Add HEALTHCHECK instruction in Dockerfile
configs.Title: No HEALTHCHECK defined

configs.Description: When using a 'FROM' statement you should use a specific tag to avoid uncontrolled behavior when the image is updated.
configs.Resolution: Add a tag to the image in the 'FROM' statement
configs.Title: ':latest' tag used

configs.Description: Running containers with 'root' user can lead to a container escape situation. It is a best practice to run containers as non-root users, which can be done by adding a 'USER' statement to the Dockerfile.
configs.Resolution: Add 'USER <non root user name>' line to the Dockerfile
configs.Title: Image user should not be 'root'

configs.Description: Exposing port 22 might allow users to SSH into the container.
configs.Resolution: Remove 'EXPOSE 22' statement from the Dockerfile
configs.Title: Port 22 exposed

configs.Description: You should use COPY instead of ADD unless you want to extract a tar file. Note that an ADD command will extract a tar file, which adds the risk of Zip-based vulnerabilities. Accordingly, it is advised to use a COPY command, which does not extract tar files.
configs.Resolution: Use COPY instead of ADD
configs.Title: ADD instead of COPY

configs.Description: COPY '--from' should not mention the current FROM alias, since it is impossible to copy from itself.
configs.Resolution: Change the '--from' so that it will not refer to itself
configs.Title: COPY '--from' referring to the current image

configs.Description: There can only be one ENTRYPOINT instruction in a Dockerfile. Only the last ENTRYPOINT instruction in the Dockerfile will have an effect.
configs.Resolution: Remove unnecessary ENTRYPOINT instruction.
configs.Title: Multiple ENTRYPOINT instructions listed

configs.Description: UNIX ports outside the range 0-65535 are exposed.
configs.Resolution: Use port number within range
configs.Title: Exposed port out of range

configs.Description: For clarity and reliability, you should always use absolute paths for your WORKDIR.
configs.Resolution: Use absolute paths for your WORKDIR
configs.Title: WORKDIR path not absolute

configs.Description: Avoid using 'RUN' with 'sudo' commands, as it can lead to unpredictable behavior.
configs.Resolution: Don't use sudo
configs.Title: RUN using 'sudo'

configs.Description: When a COPY command has more than two arguments, the last one should end with a slash.
configs.Resolution: Add slash to last COPY argument
configs.Title: COPY with more than two arguments not ending with slash

configs.Description: Different FROMs can't have the same alias defined.
configs.Resolution: Change aliases to make them different
configs.Title: Duplicate aliases defined in different FROMs

configs.Description: Use WORKDIR instead of proliferating instructions like 'RUN cd … && do-something', which are hard to read, troubleshoot, and maintain.
configs.Resolution: Use WORKDIR to change directory
configs.Title: 'RUN cd ...' to change directory

configs.Description: Avoid using both 'wget' and 'curl' since these tools have the same effect.
configs.Resolution: Pick one util, either 'wget' or 'curl'
configs.Title: RUN using 'wget' and 'curl'

configs.Description: You should use 'yum clean all' after using a 'yum install' command to clean package cached data and reduce image size.
configs.Resolution: Add 'yum clean all' to Dockerfile
configs.Title: 'yum clean all' missing

configs.Description: There can only be one CMD instruction in a Dockerfile. If you list more than one CMD then only the last CMD will take effect.
configs.Resolution: Dockerfile should only have one CMD instruction. Remove all the other CMD instructions
configs.Title: Multiple CMD instructions listed

configs.Description: The instruction 'RUN <package-manager> update' should always be followed by '<package-manager> install' in the same RUN statement.
configs.Resolution: Combine '<package-manager> update' and '<package-manager> install' instructions to single one
configs.Title: 'RUN <package-manager> update' instruction alone

configs.Description: Cached package data should be cleaned after installation to reduce image size.
configs.Resolution: Add 'dnf clean all' to Dockerfile
configs.Title: 'dnf clean all' missing

configs.Description: The layer and image size should be reduced by deleting unneeded caches after running zypper.
configs.Resolution: Add 'zypper clean' to Dockerfile
configs.Title: 'zypper clean' missing

configs.Resolution: Add '-y' flag to 'apt-get'
configs.Title: 'apt-get' missing '-y' to avoid manual input

configs.Title: Deprecated MAINTAINER used

configs.Resolution: Add '--no-cache' to 'apk add' in Dockerfile
configs.Title: 'apk add' is missing '--no-cache'

configs.Description: Cached package data should be cleaned after installation to reduce image size.
configs.Resolution: Add 'microdnf clean all' to Dockerfile
configs.Title: 'microdnf clean all' missing

configs.Description: 'apt-get' install should use '--no-install-recommends' to minimize image size.
configs.Resolution: Add '--no-install-recommends' flag to 'apt-get'
configs.Title: 'apt-get' missing '--no-install-recommends'

configs.Description: WORKDIR should not be mounted on system directories to avoid container breakouts
configs.Resolution: Avoid using system directories to mount WORKDIR
configs.Title: WORKDIR should not be mounted on system dirs

configs.Description: Passing secrets via `build-args` or envs or copying secret files can leak them out
configs.Resolution: Use secret mount if secrets are needed during image build. Use volume mount if secret files are needed during container runtime.
configs.Title: Secrets passed via `build-args` or envs or copied secret files

configs.Description:
Sources that aren't covered by the aggregator are not include in the configuration. The configuration aggregator should be configured with all_regions for the source.
This will help limit the risk of any unmonitored configuration in regions that are thought to be unused.
configs.Resolution: Set the aggregator to cover all regions
configs.Title: Config configuration aggregator should be using all regions for source

configs.Description:
IAM account password policies should prevent the reuse of passwords.

The account password policy should be set to prevent using any of the last five used passwords.
configs.Resolution: Prevent password reuse in the policy
configs.Title: IAM Password policy should prevent password reuse.

configs.Description: IAM account password policies should ensure that passwords content including at least one lowercase character.
configs.Resolution: Enforce longer, more complex passwords in the policy
configs.Title: IAM Password policy should have requirement for at least one lowercase character.

configs.Description: IAM account password policies should ensure that passwords content including at least one number.
configs.Resolution: Enforce longer, more complex passwords in the policy
configs.Title: IAM Password policy should have requirement for at least one number in the password.

configs.Description: IAM account password policies should ensure that passwords content including a symbol.
configs.Resolution: Enforce longer, more complex passwords in the policy
configs.Title: IAM Password policy should have requirement for at least one symbol in the password.

configs.Description:
,

IAM account password policies should ensure that passwords content including at least one uppercase character.
configs.Resolution: Enforce longer, more complex passwords in the policy
configs.Title: IAM Password policy should have requirement for at least one uppercase character.

configs.Description:
IAM account password policies should have a maximum age specified.

The account password policy should be set to expire passwords after 90 days or less.
configs.Resolution: Limit the password duration with an expiry in the policy
configs.Title: IAM Password policy should have expiry less than or equal to 90 days.

configs.Description:
IAM account password policies should ensure that passwords have a minimum length.

The account password policy should be set to enforce minimum password length of at least 14 characters.
configs.Resolution: Enforce longer, more complex passwords in the policy
configs.Title: IAM Password policy should have minimum password length of 14 or more characters.

configs.Description: IAM groups should be protected with multi factor authentication to add safe guards to password compromise.
configs.Resolution: Use terraform-module/enforce-mfa/aws to ensure that MFA is enforced
configs.Title: IAM groups should have MFA enforcement activated.

configs.Description: The root user has unrestricted access to all services and resources in an AWS account. We highly recommend that you avoid using the root user for daily tasks. Minimizing the use of the root user and adopting the principle of least privilege for access management reduce the risk of accidental changes and unintended disclosure of highly privileged credentials.
configs.Resolution: Use lower privileged accounts instead, so only required privileges are available.
configs.Title: The "root" account has unrestricted access to all resources in the AWS account. It is highly recommended that the use of this account be avoided.

configs.Description: CIS recommends that all access keys be associated with the root user be removed. Removing access keys associated with the root user limits vectors that the account can be compromised by. Removing the root user access keys also encourages the creation and use of role-based accounts that are least privileged.
configs.Resolution: Use lower privileged accounts instead, so only required privileges are available.
configs.Title: The root user has complete access to all services and resources in an AWS account. AWS Access Keys provide programmatic access to a given account.

configs.Description: CIS recommends that you apply IAM policies directly to groups and roles but not users. Assigning privileges at the group or role level reduces the complexity of access management as the number of users grow. Reducing access management complexity might in turn reduce opportunity for a principal to inadvertently receive or retain excessive privileges.
configs.Resolution: Grant policies at the group level instead.
configs.Title: IAM policies should not be granted directly to users.

configs.Description: Ensures any IAM pass role attached to roles are flagged and warned.
configs.Resolution: Resolve permission escalations by denying pass role'
configs.Title: IAM Pass Role Filtering

configs.Description: Ensure that the creation of the unrestricted S3 IAM policies is disallowed.
configs.Resolution: Create more restrictive S3 policies
configs.Title: Disallow unrestricted S3 IAM Policies

configs.Description: Unnecessary access to S3 buckets can lead to unauthorized access or information disclosure.
configs.Resolution: Allowing Get* along with Put* on all Resources can potentially allow unauthorized access and/or information disclosure from sensitive buckets.
configs.Title: Reduce unnecessary unauthorized access or information disclosure of S3 buckets.

configs.Description:
Storage container public access should be off. It can be configured for blobs only, containers and blobs or off entirely. The default is off, with no public access.
Explicitly overriding publicAccess to anything other than off should be avoided.
configs.Resolution: Disable public access to storage containers
configs.Title: Storage containers in blob storage mode should not have public access

configs.Description:
You can configure your storage account to accept requests from secure connections only by setting the Secure transfer required property for the storage account.
When you require secure transfer, any requests originating from an insecure connection are rejected.
Microsoft recommends that you always require secure transfer for all of your storage accounts.
configs.Resolution: Only allow secure connection for transferring data into storage accounts
configs.Title: Storage accounts should be configured to only accept transfers that are over secure connections

configs.Description:
Storage Analytics logs detailed information about successful and failed requests to a storage service.
This information can be used to monitor individual requests and to diagnose issues with a storage service.
Requests are logged on a best-effort basis.
configs.Resolution: Enable logging for Queue Services
configs.Title: When using Queue Services for a storage account, logging should be enabled.

configs.Description:
Some Microsoft services that interact with storage accounts operate from networks that can't be granted access through network rules.
To help this type of service work as intended, allow the set of trusted Microsoft services to bypass the network rules
configs.Resolution: Allow Trusted Microsoft Services to bypass
configs.Title: Trusted Microsoft Services should have bypass access to Storage accounts

configs.Description:
Azure Storage currently supports three versions of the TLS protocol: 1.0, 1.1, and 1.2.
Azure Storage uses TLS 1.2 on public HTTPS endpoints, but TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 are still supported for backward compatibility.
This check will warn if the minimum TLS is not set to TLS1_2.
configs.Resolution: Use a more recent TLS/SSL policy for the load balancer
configs.Title: The minimum TLS version for Storage Accounts should be TLS1_2

configs.Description:
The default_action for network rules should come into effect when no other rules are matched.
The default action should be set to Deny.
configs.Resolution: Set network rules to deny
configs.Title: The default action on Storage account network rules should be set to deny

configs.Description: Opening up ports to allow connections from the public internet is generally to be avoided. You should restrict access to IP addresses or ranges that are explicitly required where possible.
configs.Resolution: Set a more restrictive cidr range
configs.Title: A firewall rule should not allow unrestricted ingress from any IP address.

configs.Description: VPC flow logs record information about all traffic, which is a vital tool in reviewing anomalous traffic.
configs.Resolution: Enable VPC flow logs
configs.Title: VPC flow logs should be enabled for all subnetworks

configs.Description: Use of project-wide SSH keys means that a compromise of any one of these key pairs can result in all instances being compromised. It is recommended to use instance-level keys.
configs.Resolution: Disable project-wide SSH keys
configs.Title: Disable project-wide SSH keys for all instances

configs.Description: Instances should not be publicly exposed to the internet
configs.Resolution: Remove public IP
configs.Title: Instances should not have public IP addresses

configs.Description: When serial port access is enabled, the access is not governed by network security rules meaning the port can be exposed publicly.
configs.Resolution: Disable serial port access
configs.Title: Disable serial port connectivity for all instances

configs.Description: Using unmanaged keys makes rotation and general management difficult.
configs.Resolution: Use managed keys
configs.Title: VM disks should be encrypted with Customer Supplied Encryption Keys

configs.Description: Using unmanaged keys makes rotation and general management difficult.
configs.Resolution: Use managed keys to encrypt disks.
configs.Title: Disks should be encrypted with customer managed encryption keys

configs.Description: Opening up ports to connect out to the public internet is generally to be avoided. You should restrict access to IP addresses or ranges that are explicitly required where possible.
configs.Resolution: Set a more restrictive cidr range
configs.Title: A firewall rule should not allow unrestricted egress to any IP address.

configs.Description: OS Login automatically revokes the relevant SSH keys when an IAM user has their access revoked.
configs.Resolution: Enable OS Login at project level and remove instance-level overrides
configs.Title: Instances should not override the project setting for OS Login

configs.Description: Sensitive values such as raw encryption keys should not be included in your Terraform code, and should be stored securely by a secrets manager.
configs.Resolution: Reference a managed key rather than include the key in raw format.
configs.Title: The encryption key used to encrypt a compute disk has been specified in plaintext.

configs.Description: TLS versions prior to 1.2 are outdated and insecure. You should use 1.2 as aminimum version.
configs.Resolution: Enforce a minimum TLS version of 1.2
configs.Title: SSL policies should enforce secure versions of TLS

configs.Description: The virtual TPM provides numerous security measures to your VM.
configs.Resolution: Enable Shielded VM VTPM
configs.Title: Instances should have Shielded VM VTPM enabled

configs.Description: OS Login automatically revokes the relevant SSH keys when an IAM user has their access revoked.
configs.Resolution: Enable OS Login at project level
configs.Title: OS Login should be enabled at project level

configs.Description: Disabling IP forwarding ensures the instance can only receive packets addressed to the instance and can only send packets with a source address of the instance.
configs.Resolution: Disable IP forwarding
configs.Title: Instances should not have IP forwarding enabled

configs.Description: The default service account has full project access. Instances should instead be assigned the minimal access they need.
configs.Resolution: Remove use of default service account
configs.Title: Instances should not use the default service account

configs.Description: Integrity monitoring helps you understand and make decisions about the state of your VM instances.
configs.Resolution: Enable Shielded VM Integrity Monitoring
configs.Title: Instances should have Shielded VM integrity monitoring enabled

configs.Description: Secure boot helps ensure that the system only runs authentic software.
configs.Resolution: Enable Shielded VM secure boot
configs.Title: Instances should have Shielded VM secure boot enabled

configs.Description: Block devices should be encrypted to ensure sensitive data is held securely at rest.
configs.Resolution: Turn on encryption for all block devices
configs.Title: Launch configuration with unencrypted block device.

configs.Description: You should limit the provision of public IP addresses for resources. Resources should not be exposed on the public internet, but should have access limited to consumers required for the function of your application.
configs.Resolution: Set the instance to not be publicly accessible
configs.Title: Launch configuration should not have a public IP address.

configs.Description:
Sources that aren't covered by the aggregator are not include in the configuration. The configuration aggregator should be configured with all_regions for the source.
This will help limit the risk of any unmonitored configuration in regions that are thought to be unused.
configs.Resolution: Set the aggregator to cover all regions
configs.Title: Config configuration aggregator should be using all regions for source

configs.Description: Allowing public access to the ECR repository risks leaking sensitive of abusable information
configs.Resolution: Do not allow public access in the policy
configs.Title: ECR repository policy must block public access

configs.Description: You should not use outdated/insecure TLS versions for encryption. You should be using TLS v1.2+.
configs.Resolution: Use a more recent TLS/SSL policy for the load balancer
configs.Title: An outdated SSL policy is in use by a load balancer.

configs.Description:
Passing unknown or invalid headers through to the target poses a potential risk of compromise.
By setting drop_invalid_header_fields to true, anything that does not conform to well known, defined headers will be removed by the load balancer.
configs.Resolution: Set drop_invalid_header_fields to true
configs.Title: Load balancers should drop invalid headers

configs.Description:
Plain HTTP is unencrypted and human-readable. This means that if a malicious actor was to eavesdrop on your connection, they would be able to see all of your data flowing back and forth.
You should use HTTPS, which is HTTP over an encrypted (TLS) connection, meaning eavesdroppers cannot read your traffic.
configs.Resolution: Switch to HTTPS to benefit from TLS security features
configs.Title: Use of plain HTTP.

configs.Title: IAM Password policy should prevent password reuse.

,

configs.Title: IAM Password policy should have requirement for at least one uppercase character.

configs.Description:
IAM account password policies should ensure that passwords have a minimum length.

configs.Resolution: Enforce longer, more complex passwords in the policy
configs.Title: IAM Password policy should have minimum password length of 14 or more characters.

configs.Description:
When the principal is an AWS service, the ARN of the specific resource within that service to grant permission to.
Without this, any resource from principal will be granted permission – even if that resource is from another account.
For S3, this should be the ARN of the S3 Bucket. For CloudWatch Events, this should be the ARN of the CloudWatch Events Rule. For API Gateway, this should be the ARN of the API
configs.Resolution: Always provide a source arn for Lambda permissions
configs.Title: Ensure that lambda function permission has a source arn specified

configs.Description:
Amazon RDS uses the AWS managed key for your new DB instance. For complete control over KMS keys, including establishing and maintaining their key policies, IAM policies, and grants, enabling and disabling them, and rotating their cryptographic material, use a customer managed keys.
The encryption key specified in `performance_insights_kms_key_id` references a KMS ARN
configs.Resolution: Use Customer Managed Keys to encrypt Performance Insights data
configs.Title: Performance Insights encryption should use Customer Managed Keys

configs.Description: Buckets should not have ACLs that allow public access
configs.Resolution: Don't use canned ACLs or switch to private acl
configs.Title: S3 Buckets not publicly accessible through ACL.

configs.Description: Default VPC does not have a lot of the critical security features that standard VPC comes with, new resources should not be created in the default VPC and it should not be present in the Terraform.
configs.Resolution: Create a non-default vpc for resources to be created in
configs.Title: AWS best practice to not use the default VPC for workflows

configs.Description: Ensure access to specific required ports is allowed, and nothing else.
configs.Resolution: Set specific allowed ports
configs.Title: An Network ACL rule allows ALL ports.

configs.Description:
The Network Access Control List (NACL) function provide stateless filtering of ingress and
egress network traffic to AWS resources. It is recommended that no NACL allows
unrestricted ingress access to remote server administration ports, such as SSH to port 22
and RDP to port 3389.
configs.Resolution: Set a more restrictive CIDR range
configs.Title: Network ACLs should not allow unrestricted ingress to SSH or RDP from any IP address.

configs.Description: When creating Launch Configurations, user data can be used for the initial configuration of the instance. User data must not contain any sensitive data.
configs.Resolution: Don't use sensitive data in user data
configs.Title: Ensure all data stored in the launch configuration EBS is securely encrypted

configs.Description: IAM groups should be protected with multi factor authentication to add safe guards to password compromise.
configs.Resolution: Use terraform-module/enforce-mfa/aws to ensure that MFA is enforced
configs.Title: IAM groups should have MFA enforcement activated.

configs.Description: EC2 instance data is used to pass start up information into the EC2 instance. This userdata must not contain access key credentials. Instead use an IAM Instance Profile assigned to the instance to grant access to other AWS Services.
configs.Resolution: Remove sensitive data from the EC2 instance user-data generated by launch templates
configs.Title: User data for EC2 instances must not contain sensitive AWS keys

You can use the S3 Versioning feature to preserve, retrieve, and restore every version of every object stored in your buckets.

configs.Description: Running containers with 'root' user can lead to a container escape situation. It is a best practice to run containers as non-root users, which can be done by adding a 'USER' statement to the Dockerfile.
configs.Message: Specify at least 1 USER command in Dockerfile with non-root user as argument
configs.Resolution: Add 'USER <non root user name>' line to the Dockerfile
configs.Title: Image user should not be 'root'

configs.Message: Add HEALTHCHECK instruction in your Dockerfile
configs.Resolution: Add HEALTHCHECK instruction in Dockerfile
configs.Title: No HEALTHCHECK defined

configs.Description: When using a 'FROM' statement you should use a specific tag to avoid uncontrolled behavior when the image is updated.
configs.Resolution: Add a tag to the image in the 'FROM' statement
configs.Title: ':latest' tag used

configs.Description: Exposing port 22 might allow users to SSH into the container.
configs.Resolution: Remove 'EXPOSE 22' statement from the Dockerfile
configs.Title: Port 22 exposed

configs.Description: You should use COPY instead of ADD unless you want to extract a tar file. Note that an ADD command will extract a tar file, which adds the risk of Zip-based vulnerabilities. Accordingly, it is advised to use a COPY command, which does not extract tar files.
configs.Resolution: Use COPY instead of ADD
configs.Title: ADD instead of COPY

configs.Description: COPY '--from' should not mention the current FROM alias, since it is impossible to copy from itself.
configs.Resolution: Change the '--from' so that it will not refer to itself
configs.Title: COPY '--from' referring to the current image

configs.Description: There can only be one ENTRYPOINT instruction in a Dockerfile. Only the last ENTRYPOINT instruction in the Dockerfile will have an effect.
configs.Resolution: Remove unnecessary ENTRYPOINT instruction.
configs.Title: Multiple ENTRYPOINT instructions listed

configs.Description: UNIX ports outside the range 0-65535 are exposed.
configs.Resolution: Use port number within range
configs.Title: Exposed port out of range

configs.Description: For clarity and reliability, you should always use absolute paths for your WORKDIR.
configs.Resolution: Use absolute paths for your WORKDIR
configs.Title: WORKDIR path not absolute

configs.Description: Avoid using 'RUN' with 'sudo' commands, as it can lead to unpredictable behavior.
configs.Resolution: Don't use sudo
configs.Title: RUN using 'sudo'

configs.Description: When a COPY command has more than two arguments, the last one should end with a slash.
configs.Resolution: Add slash to last COPY argument
configs.Title: COPY with more than two arguments not ending with slash

configs.Description: Different FROMs can't have the same alias defined.
configs.Resolution: Change aliases to make them different
configs.Title: Duplicate aliases defined in different FROMs

configs.Description: Use WORKDIR instead of proliferating instructions like 'RUN cd … && do-something', which are hard to read, troubleshoot, and maintain.
configs.Resolution: Use WORKDIR to change directory
configs.Title: 'RUN cd ...' to change directory

configs.Description: Avoid using both 'wget' and 'curl' since these tools have the same effect.
configs.Resolution: Pick one util, either 'wget' or 'curl'
configs.Title: RUN using 'wget' and 'curl'

configs.Description: You should use 'yum clean all' after using a 'yum install' command to clean package cached data and reduce image size.
configs.Resolution: Add 'yum clean all' to Dockerfile
configs.Title: 'yum clean all' missing

configs.Description: There can only be one CMD instruction in a Dockerfile. If you list more than one CMD then only the last CMD will take effect.
configs.Resolution: Dockerfile should only have one CMD instruction. Remove all the other CMD instructions
configs.Title: Multiple CMD instructions listed

configs.Description: The instruction 'RUN <package-manager> update' should always be followed by '<package-manager> install' in the same RUN statement.
configs.Resolution: Combine '<package-manager> update' and '<package-manager> install' instructions to single one
configs.Title: 'RUN <package-manager> update' instruction alone

configs.Description: Cached package data should be cleaned after installation to reduce image size.
configs.Resolution: Add 'dnf clean all' to Dockerfile
configs.Title: 'dnf clean all' missing

configs.Description: The layer and image size should be reduced by deleting unneeded caches after running zypper.
configs.Resolution: Add 'zypper clean' to Dockerfile
configs.Title: 'zypper clean' missing

configs.Description: 'apt-get' calls should use the flag '-y' to avoid manual user input.
configs.Resolution: Add '-y' flag to 'apt-get'
configs.Title: 'apt-get' missing '-y' to avoid manual input

configs.Description: MAINTAINER has been deprecated since Docker 1.13.0.
configs.Resolution: Use LABEL instead of MAINTAINER
configs.Title: Deprecated MAINTAINER used

configs.Description: Providing more than one HEALTHCHECK instruction per stage is confusing and error-prone.
configs.Resolution: One HEALTHCHECK instruction must remain in Dockerfile. Remove all other instructions.
configs.Title: Multiple HEALTHCHECK defined

configs.Description: You should use 'apk add' with '--no-cache' to clean package cached data and reduce image size.
configs.Resolution: Add '--no-cache' to 'apk add' in Dockerfile
configs.Title: 'apk add' is missing '--no-cache'

configs.Description: Cached package data should be cleaned after installation to reduce image size.
configs.Resolution: Add 'microdnf clean all' to Dockerfile
configs.Title: 'microdnf clean all' missing

configs.Description: 'apt-get' install should use '--no-install-recommends' to minimize image size.
configs.Resolution: Add '--no-install-recommends' flag to 'apt-get'
configs.Title: 'apt-get' missing '--no-install-recommends'

configs.Description: WORKDIR should not be mounted on system directories to avoid container breakouts
configs.Resolution: Avoid using system directories to mount WORKDIR
configs.Title: WORKDIR should not be mounted on system dirs

configs.Description: Passing secrets via `build-args` or envs or copying secret files can leak them out
configs.Resolution: Use secret mount if secrets are needed during image build. Use volume mount if secret files are needed during container runtime.
configs.Title: Secrets passed via `build-args` or envs or copied secret files

configs.Description:
Versioning in Amazon S3 is a means of keeping multiple variants of an object in the same bucket.

You can use the S3 Versioning feature to preserve, retrieve, and restore every version of every object stored in your buckets.

With versioning you can recover more easily from both unintended user actions and application failures.

When you enable versioning, also keep in mind the potential costs of storing noncurrent versions of objects. To help manage those costs, consider setting up an S3 Lifecycle configuration.
configs.Message: Bucket does not have versioning enabled
configs.Resolution: Enable versioning to protect against accidental/malicious removal or modification
configs.Title: S3 Data should be versioned

configs.Resolution: Use SSE-KMS with a customer managed key (CMK)
configs.Title: S3 encryption should use Customer Managed Keys

,

configs.Title: IAM groups should have MFA enforcement activated.

configs.Description: The root user has unrestricted access to all services and resources in an AWS account. We highly recommend that you avoid using the root user for daily tasks. Minimizing the use of the root user and adopting the principle of least privilege for access management reduce the risk of accidental changes and unintended disclosure of highly privileged credentials.
configs.Resolution: Use lower privileged accounts instead, so only required privileges are available.
configs.Title: The "root" account has unrestricted access to all resources in the AWS account. It is highly recommended that the use of this account be avoided.

configs.Description: CIS recommends that all access keys be associated with the root user be removed. Removing access keys associated with the root user limits vectors that the account can be compromised by. Removing the root user access keys also encourages the creation and use of role-based accounts that are least privileged.
configs.Resolution: Use lower privileged accounts instead, so only required privileges are available.
configs.Title: The root user has complete access to all services and resources in an AWS account. AWS Access Keys provide programmatic access to a given account.

configs.Description: CIS recommends that you apply IAM policies directly to groups and roles but not users. Assigning privileges at the group or role level reduces the complexity of access management as the number of users grow. Reducing access management complexity might in turn reduce opportunity for a principal to inadvertently receive or retain excessive privileges.
configs.Resolution: Grant policies at the group level instead.
configs.Title: IAM policies should not be granted directly to users.

configs.Description: Ensures any IAM pass role attached to roles are flagged and warned.
configs.Resolution: Resolve permission escalations by denying pass role'
configs.Title: IAM Pass Role Filtering

configs.Description: Ensure that the creation of the unrestricted S3 IAM policies is disallowed.
configs.Resolution: Create more restrictive S3 policies
configs.Title: Disallow unrestricted S3 IAM Policies

configs.Description: Unnecessary access to S3 buckets can lead to unauthorized access or information disclosure.
configs.Resolution: Allowing Get* along with Put* on all Resources can potentially allow unauthorized access and/or information disclosure from sensitive buckets.
configs.Title: Reduce unnecessary unauthorized access or information disclosure of S3 buckets.

configs.Description: By default, clients can connect to function endpoints by using both HTTP or HTTPS. You should redirect HTTP to HTTPs because HTTPS uses the SSL/TLS protocol to provide a secure connection, which is both encrypted and authenticated.
configs.Resolution: You can redirect all HTTP requests to the HTTPS port.
configs.Title: Ensure the Function App can only be accessed via HTTPS. The default is false.

configs.Description:
Storage container public access should be off. It can be configured for blobs only, containers and blobs or off entirely. The default is off, with no public access.
Explicitly overriding publicAccess to anything other than off should be avoided.
configs.Resolution: Disable public access to storage containers
configs.Title: Storage containers in blob storage mode should not have public access

configs.Description:
You can configure your storage account to accept requests from secure connections only by setting the Secure transfer required property for the storage account.
When you require secure transfer, any requests originating from an insecure connection are rejected.
Microsoft recommends that you always require secure transfer for all of your storage accounts.
configs.Resolution: Only allow secure connection for transferring data into storage accounts
configs.Title: Storage accounts should be configured to only accept transfers that are over secure connections

configs.Description:
Storage Analytics logs detailed information about successful and failed requests to a storage service.
This information can be used to monitor individual requests and to diagnose issues with a storage service.
Requests are logged on a best-effort basis.
configs.Resolution: Enable logging for Queue Services
configs.Title: When using Queue Services for a storage account, logging should be enabled.

configs.Description:
Azure Storage currently supports three versions of the TLS protocol: 1.0, 1.1, and 1.2.
Azure Storage uses TLS 1.2 on public HTTPS endpoints, but TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 are still supported for backward compatibility.
This check will warn if the minimum TLS is not set to TLS1_2.
configs.Resolution: Use a more recent TLS/SSL policy for the load balancer
configs.Title: The minimum TLS version for Storage Accounts should be TLS1_2

configs.Description:
The default_action for network rules should come into effect when no other rules are matched.
The default action should be set to Deny.
configs.Resolution: Set network rules to deny
configs.Title: The default action on Storage account network rules should be set to deny

configs.Description:
When Auditing is configured for a SQL database, if the retention period is not set, the retention will be unlimited.

If the retention period is to be explicitly set, it should be set for no less than 90 days.
configs.Resolution: Set retention periods of database auditing to greater than 90 days
configs.Title: Database auditing retention period should be longer than 90 days

configs.Description: Azure services can be allowed access through the firewall using a start and end IP address of 0.0.0.0. No other end ip address should be combined with a start of 0.0.0.0

configs.Description: The permissions granted to a role should be kept to the minimum required to be able to do the task. Wildcard permissions must not be used.

configs.Description:
Content Type is an optional Key Vault Secret behavior and is not enabled by default.

Clients may specify the content type of a secret to assist in interpreting the secret data when it's retrieved. The maximum length of this field is 255 characters. There are no pre-defined values. The suggested usage is as a hint for interpreting the secret data.

configs.Description: Subscription owners should be notified when there are security alerts. By ensuring the administrators of the account have been notified they can quickly assist in any required remediation
configs.Message: Security alert policy does not alert account admins.
configs.Resolution: Enable email to subscription owners
configs.Title: Security threat alerts go to subscription owners and co-administrators

configs.Description: Postgresql can generate logs for checkpoints to improve visibility for audit and configuration issue resolution.
configs.Message: Database server is not configured to log checkpoints.
configs.Resolution: Enable checkpoint logging
configs.Title: Ensure server parameter 'log_checkpoints' is set to 'ON' for PostgreSQL Database Server

configs.Description: You should not use outdated/insecure TLS versions for encryption. You should be using TLS v1.2+.
configs.Message: Database server does not require a secure TLS version.
configs.Resolution: Use the most modern TLS policies available
configs.Title: Databases should have the minimum TLS set for connections

configs.Description: You should not use outdated/insecure TLS versions for encryption. You should be using TLS v1.2+.
configs.Message: Database server does not require a secure TLS version.
configs.Resolution: Use the most modern TLS policies available

configs.Description: You should not use outdated/insecure TLS versions for encryption. You should be using TLS v1.2+.
configs.Message: Database server does not require a secure TLS version.
configs.Resolution: Use the most modern TLS policies available
configs.Title: Databases should have the minimum TLS set for connections

configs.Description: Auditing helps you maintain regulatory compliance, understand database activity, and gain insight into discrepancies and anomalies that could indicate business concerns or suspected security violations.
configs.Message: Server does not have an extended audit policy configured.
configs.Resolution: Enable auditing on Azure SQL databases
configs.Title: Auditing should be enabled on Azure SQL Databases

configs.Description: SQL Server can alert for security issues including SQL Injection, vulnerabilities, access anomalies and data exfiltration. Ensure none of these are disabled to benefit from the best protection
configs.Message: Server has a security alert policy which disables alerts.
configs.Resolution: Use all provided threat alerts
configs.Title: No threat detections are set

configs.Description:
Some Microsoft services that interact with storage accounts operate from networks that can't be granted access through network rules.
To help this type of service work as intended, allow the set of trusted Microsoft services to bypass the network rules
configs.Message: Network rules do not allow bypass for Microsoft Services.
configs.Resolution: Allow Trusted Microsoft Services to bypass
configs.Title: Trusted Microsoft Services should have bypass access to Storage accounts

configs.Description: Manage disks should be encrypted at rest. When specifying the encryption_settings block, the enabled attribute should be set to true.
configs.Resolution: Enable encryption on managed disks
configs.Title: Enable disk encryption on managed disk

configs.Description:
Sources that aren't covered by the aggregator are not include in the configuration. The configuration aggregator should be configured with all_regions for the source.
This will help limit the risk of any unmonitored configuration in regions that are thought to be unused.
configs.Resolution: Set the aggregator to cover all regions
configs.Title: Config configuration aggregator should be using all regions for source

configs.Description:
IAM account password policies should prevent the reuse of passwords.

The account password policy should be set to prevent using any of the last five used passwords.
configs.Resolution: Prevent password reuse in the policy
configs.Title: IAM Password policy should prevent password reuse.

configs.Description: IAM account password policies should ensure that passwords content including at least one lowercase character.
configs.Resolution: Enforce longer, more complex passwords in the policy
configs.Title: IAM Password policy should have requirement for at least one lowercase character.

configs.Description: IAM account password policies should ensure that passwords content including at least one number.
configs.Resolution: Enforce longer, more complex passwords in the policy
configs.Title: IAM Password policy should have requirement for at least one number in the password.

configs.Description: IAM account password policies should ensure that passwords content including a symbol.
configs.Resolution: Enforce longer, more complex passwords in the policy
configs.Title: IAM Password policy should have requirement for at least one symbol in the password.

configs.Description:
,

IAM account password policies should ensure that passwords content including at least one uppercase character.
configs.Resolution: Enforce longer, more complex passwords in the policy
configs.Title: IAM Password policy should have requirement for at least one uppercase character.

configs.Description:
IAM account password policies should have a maximum age specified.

The account password policy should be set to expire passwords after 90 days or less.
configs.Resolution: Limit the password duration with an expiry in the policy
configs.Title: IAM Password policy should have expiry less than or equal to 90 days.

configs.Description:
IAM account password policies should ensure that passwords have a minimum length.

The account password policy should be set to enforce minimum password length of at least 14 characters.
configs.Resolution: Enforce longer, more complex passwords in the policy
configs.Title: IAM Password policy should have minimum password length of 14 or more characters.

configs.Description: IAM groups should be protected with multi factor authentication to add safe guards to password compromise.
configs.Resolution: Use terraform-module/enforce-mfa/aws to ensure that MFA is enforced
configs.Title: IAM groups should have MFA enforcement activated.

configs.Description: The root user has unrestricted access to all services and resources in an AWS account. We highly recommend that you avoid using the root user for daily tasks. Minimizing the use of the root user and adopting the principle of least privilege for access management reduce the risk of accidental changes and unintended disclosure of highly privileged credentials.
configs.Resolution: Use lower privileged accounts instead, so only required privileges are available.
configs.Title: The "root" account has unrestricted access to all resources in the AWS account. It is highly recommended that the use of this account be avoided.

configs.Description: CIS recommends that all access keys be associated with the root user be removed. Removing access keys associated with the root user limits vectors that the account can be compromised by. Removing the root user access keys also encourages the creation and use of role-based accounts that are least privileged.
configs.Resolution: Use lower privileged accounts instead, so only required privileges are available.
configs.Title: The root user has complete access to all services and resources in an AWS account. AWS Access Keys provide programmatic access to a given account.

configs.Description: CIS recommends that you apply IAM policies directly to groups and roles but not users. Assigning privileges at the group or role level reduces the complexity of access management as the number of users grow. Reducing access management complexity might in turn reduce opportunity for a principal to inadvertently receive or retain excessive privileges.
configs.Resolution: Grant policies at the group level instead.
configs.Title: IAM policies should not be granted directly to users.

configs.Description: Ensures any IAM pass role attached to roles are flagged and warned.
configs.Resolution: Resolve permission escalations by denying pass role'
configs.Title: IAM Pass Role Filtering

configs.Description: Ensure that the creation of the unrestricted S3 IAM policies is disallowed.
configs.Resolution: Create more restrictive S3 policies
configs.Title: Disallow unrestricted S3 IAM Policies

configs.Description: Unnecessary access to S3 buckets can lead to unauthorized access or information disclosure.
configs.Resolution: Allowing Get* along with Put* on all Resources can potentially allow unauthorized access and/or information disclosure from sensitive buckets.
configs.Title: Reduce unnecessary unauthorized access or information disclosure of S3 buckets.

configs.Description:
Storage container public access should be off. It can be configured for blobs only, containers and blobs or off entirely. The default is off, with no public access.
Explicitly overriding publicAccess to anything other than off should be avoided.
configs.Resolution: Disable public access to storage containers
configs.Title: Storage containers in blob storage mode should not have public access

configs.Description:
You can configure your storage account to accept requests from secure connections only by setting the Secure transfer required property for the storage account.
When you require secure transfer, any requests originating from an insecure connection are rejected.
Microsoft recommends that you always require secure transfer for all of your storage accounts.
configs.Resolution: Only allow secure connection for transferring data into storage accounts
configs.Title: Storage accounts should be configured to only accept transfers that are over secure connections

configs.Description:
Storage Analytics logs detailed information about successful and failed requests to a storage service.
This information can be used to monitor individual requests and to diagnose issues with a storage service.
Requests are logged on a best-effort basis.
configs.Resolution: Enable logging for Queue Services
configs.Title: When using Queue Services for a storage account, logging should be enabled.

configs.Description:
Some Microsoft services that interact with storage accounts operate from networks that can't be granted access through network rules.
To help this type of service work as intended, allow the set of trusted Microsoft services to bypass the network rules
configs.Resolution: Allow Trusted Microsoft Services to bypass
configs.Title: Trusted Microsoft Services should have bypass access to Storage accounts

configs.Description:
Azure Storage currently supports three versions of the TLS protocol: 1.0, 1.1, and 1.2.
Azure Storage uses TLS 1.2 on public HTTPS endpoints, but TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 are still supported for backward compatibility.
This check will warn if the minimum TLS is not set to TLS1_2.
configs.Resolution: Use a more recent TLS/SSL policy for the load balancer
configs.Title: The minimum TLS version for Storage Accounts should be TLS1_2

configs.Description:
The default_action for network rules should come into effect when no other rules are matched.
The default action should be set to Deny.
configs.Resolution: Set network rules to deny
configs.Title: The default action on Storage account network rules should be set to deny

configs.Description:
Permissions should not be directly granted to users, you identify roles that contain the appropriate permissions, and then grant those roles to the user.

Granting permissions to users quickly become unwieldy and complex to make large scale changes to remove access to a particular resource.

Permissions should be granted on roles, groups, services accounts instead.
configs.Resolution: Roles should be granted permissions and assigned to users
configs.Title: IAM granted directly to user.

configs.Description: Default service accounts should not be used - consider creating specialised service accounts for individual purposes.
configs.Resolution: Use specialised service accounts for specific purposes.
configs.Title: Roles should not be assigned to default service accounts

configs.Description: Users with service account access at folder level can impersonate any service account. Instead, they should be given access to particular service accounts as required.
configs.Resolution: Provide access at the service-level instead of folder-level, if required
configs.Title: Users should not be granted service account access at the folder level

configs.Description: Default service accounts should not be used - consider creating specialised service accounts for individual purposes.
configs.Resolution: Use specialised service accounts for specific purposes.
configs.Title: Roles should not be assigned to default service accounts

configs.Description: Service accounts should have a minimal set of permissions assigned in order to do their job. They should never have excessive access as if compromised, an attacker can escalate privileges and take over the entire account.
configs.Resolution: Limit service account access to minimal required set
configs.Title: Service accounts should not have roles assigned with excessive privileges

configs.Description: Default service accounts should not be used - consider creating specialised service accounts for individual purposes.
configs.Resolution: Use specialised service accounts for specific purposes.
configs.Title: Roles should not be assigned to default service accounts

configs.Description: Users with service account access at organization level can impersonate any service account. Instead, they should be given access to particular service accounts as required.
configs.Resolution: Provide access at the service-level instead of organization-level, if required
configs.Title: Users should not be granted service account access at the organization level

configs.Description: The default network which is provided for a project contains multiple insecure firewall rules which allow ingress to the project's infrastructure. Creation of this network should therefore be disabled.
configs.Resolution: Disable automatic default network creation
configs.Title: Default network should not be created at project level

configs.Description: Users with service account access at project level can impersonate any service account. Instead, they should be given access to particular service accounts as required.
configs.Resolution: Provide access at the service-level instead of project-level, if required
configs.Title: Users should not be granted service account access at the project level

configs.Description: Setting the minimum log severity too high will cause errors not to be logged
configs.Resolution: Set the minimum log severity to at least ERROR
configs.Title: Ensure that Postgres errors are logged

configs.Description: Cross-database ownership chaining, also known as cross-database chaining, is a security feature of SQL Server that allows users of databases access to other databases besides the one they are currently using.
configs.Resolution: Disable cross database ownership chaining
configs.Title: Cross-database ownership chaining should be disabled

configs.Description: Logging of statements which could contain sensitive data is not advised, therefore this setting should preclude all statements from being logged.
configs.Resolution: Disable minimum duration statement logging completely
configs.Title: Ensure that logging of long statements is disabled.

configs.Description: Users with ALTER permissions on users can grant access to a contained database without the knowledge of an administrator
configs.Resolution: Disable contained database authentication
configs.Title: Contained database authentication should be disabled

configs.Description: Arbitrary files can be read from the system using LOAD_DATA unless this setting is disabled.
configs.Resolution: Disable the local infile setting
configs.Title: Disable local_infile setting in MySQL

configs.Description: Opening up ports to connect out to the public internet is generally to be avoided. You should restrict access to IP addresses or ranges that are explicitly required where possible.
configs.Resolution: Set a more restrictive cidr range
configs.Title: A firewall rule should not allow unrestricted egress to any IP address.

configs.Description: Sensitive values such as raw encryption keys should not be included in your Terraform code, and should be stored securely by a secrets manager.
configs.Resolution: Reference a managed key rather than include the key in raw format.
configs.Title: The encryption key used to encrypt a compute disk has been specified in plaintext.

configs.Description: TLS versions prior to 1.2 are outdated and insecure. You should use 1.2 as aminimum version.
configs.Resolution: Enforce a minimum TLS version of 1.2
configs.Title: SSL policies should enforce secure versions of TLS

configs.Description: OS Login automatically revokes the relevant SSH keys when an IAM user has their access revoked.
configs.Resolution: Enable OS Login at project level
configs.Title: OS Login should be enabled at project level

configs.Description: The default service account has full project access. Instances should instead be assigned the minimal access they need.
configs.Resolution: Remove use of default service account
configs.Title: Instances should not use the default service account

configs.Description:
CIS GKE Benchmark Recommendation: 6.5.5. Ensure Shielded GKE Nodes are Enabled

Shielded GKE Nodes provide strong, verifiable node identity and integrity to increase the security of GKE nodes and should be enabled on all GKE clusters.
configs.Resolution: Enable node shielding
configs.Title: Shielded GKE nodes not enabled.

configs.Description: Enabling authorized networks means you can restrict master access to a fixed set of CIDR ranges
configs.Resolution: Enable master authorized networks
configs.Title: Master authorized networks should be configured on GKE clusters

configs.Description: It is recommended to use Service Accounts and OAuth as authentication methods for accessing the master in the container cluster. Basic authentication should be disabled by explicitly unsetting the username and password on the master_auth block.
configs.Resolution: Use service account or OAuth for authentication
configs.Title: Legacy client authentication methods utilized.

configs.Description: In GitHub Actions, one can authenticate to Google Cloud by setting values for workload_identity_provider and service_account and requesting a short-lived OIDC token which is then used to execute commands as that Service Account. If you don't specify a condition in the workload identity provider pool configuration, then any GitHub Action can assume this role and act as that Service Account.
configs.Resolution: Set conditions on this provider, for example by restricting it to only be allowed from repositories in your GitHub organization
configs.Title: A configuration for an external workload identity pool provider should have conditions set

configs.Description: Temporary files are not logged by default. To log all temporary files, a value of `0` should set in the `log_temp_files` flag - as all files greater in size than the number of bytes set in this flag will be logged.
configs.Message: Database instance has temporary file logging disabled.
configs.Resolution: Enable temporary file logging for all files
configs.Title: Temporary file logging should be enabled for all temporary files.

configs.Description: In-transit data should be encrypted so that if traffic is intercepted data will not be exposed in plaintext to attackers.
configs.Message: Database instance does not require TLS for all connections.
configs.Resolution: Enforce SSL for all connections
configs.Title: SSL connections to a SQL database instance should be enforced.

configs.Description: Logging connections provides useful diagnostic data such as session length, which can identify performance issues in an application and potential DoS vectors.
configs.Message: Database instance is not configured to log connections.
configs.Resolution: Enable connection logging.
configs.Title: Ensure that logging of connections is enabled.

configs.Description: Database instances should be configured so that they are not available over the public internet, but to internal compute resources which access them.
configs.Message: Database instance allows access from the public internet.
configs.Resolution: Remove public access from database instances
configs.Title: Ensure that Cloud SQL Database Instances are not publicly exposed

configs.Description: Database instances should be configured so that they are not available over the public internet, but to internal compute resources which access them.
configs.Message: Database instance is granted a public internet address.
configs.Resolution: Remove public access from database instances
configs.Title: Ensure that Cloud SQL Database Instances are not publicly exposed

configs.Description: Lock waits are often an indication of poor performance and often an indicator of a potential denial of service vulnerability, therefore occurrences should be logged for analysis.
configs.Message: Database instance is not configured to log lock waits.
configs.Resolution: Enable lock wait logging.
configs.Title: Ensure that logging of lock waits is enabled.

configs.Description: Logging disconnections provides useful diagnostic data such as session length, which can identify performance issues in an application and potential DoS vectors.
configs.Message: Database instance is not configured to log disconnections.
configs.Resolution: Enable disconnection logging.
configs.Title: Ensure that logging of disconnections is enabled.

configs.Description: Automated backups are not enabled by default. Backups are an easy way to restore data in a corruption or data-loss scenario.
configs.Message: Database instance does not have backups enabled.
configs.Resolution: Enable automated backups
configs.Title: Enable automated backups to recover from data-loss

configs.Description: Logging checkpoints provides useful diagnostic data, which can identify performance issues in an application and potential DoS vectors.
configs.Message: Database instance is not configured to log checkpoints.
configs.Resolution: Enable checkpoints logging.
configs.Title: Ensure that logging of checkpoints is enabled.

configs.Description: Using 'allAuthenticatedUsers' provides any GCP user - even those outside of your organisation - access to your BigQuery dataset.
configs.Message: Dataset grants access to all authenticated GCP users.
configs.Resolution: Configure access permissions with higher granularity
configs.Title: BigQuery datasets should only be accessible within the organisation

configs.Description: Using 'allUsers' or 'allAuthenticatedUsers' as members in an IAM member/binding causes data to be exposed outside of the organisation.
configs.Resolution: Restrict public access to the bucket.
configs.Title: Ensure that Cloud Storage bucket is not anonymously or publicly accessible.

configs.Description: When you enable uniform bucket-level access on a bucket, Access Control Lists (ACLs) are disabled, and only bucket-level Identity and Access Management (IAM) permissions grant access to that bucket and the objects it contains. You revoke all access granted by object ACLs and the ability to administrate permissions using bucket ACLs.
configs.Message: Bucket has uniform bucket level access disabled.
configs.Resolution: Enable uniform bucket level access to provide a uniform permissioning system.
configs.Title: Ensure that Cloud Storage buckets have uniform bucket-level access enabled

configs.Description: Using unmanaged keys makes rotation and general management difficult.
configs.Message: Storage bucket encryption does not use a customer-managed key.
configs.Resolution: Encrypt Cloud Storage buckets using customer-managed keys.
configs.Title: Cloud Storage buckets should be encrypted with a customer-managed key.

configs.Description: The Compute Engine instance metadata server exposes legacy v0.1 and v1beta1 endpoints, which do not enforce metadata query headers. This is a feature in the v1 APIs that makes it more difficult for a potential attacker to retrieve instance metadata. Unless specifically required, we recommend you disable these legacy APIs. When setting the metadata block, the default value for disable-legacy-endpoints is set to true, they should not be explicitly enabled.
configs.Message: Cluster has legacy metadata endpoints enabled.
configs.Resolution: Disable legacy metadata endpoints
configs.Title: Legacy metadata endpoints enabled.

configs.Description: IP aliasing allows the reuse of public IPs internally, removing the need for a NAT gateway.
configs.Resolution: Enable IP aliasing
configs.Title: Clusters should have IP aliasing enabled

configs.Description: You should create and use a minimally privileged service account to run your GKE cluster instead of using the Compute Engine default service account.
configs.Message: Node pool does not override the default service account.

configs.Description: Labels make it easier to manage assets and differentiate between clusters and environments, allowing the mapping of computational resources to the wider organisational structure.
configs.Message: Cluster does not use GCE resource labels.
configs.Resolution: Set cluster resource labels
configs.Title: Clusters should be configured with Labels

configs.Description: StackDriver monitoring aggregates logs, events, and metrics from your Kubernetes environment on GKE to help you understand your application's behavior in production.
configs.Message: Cluster does not use the monitoring.googleapis.com/kubernetes StackDriver monitoring service.
configs.Resolution: Enable StackDriver monitoring
configs.Title: Stackdriver Monitoring should be enabled

configs.Description: The GKE control plane is exposed to the public internet by default.

configs.Resolution: Use private nodes and master authorised networks to prevent exposure

configs.Description: GKE supports several OS image types but COS is the recommended OS image to use on cluster nodes for enhanced security

configs.Resolution: Use the COS image type

configs.Resolution: Enable network policy
configs.Title: Network Policy should be enabled on GKE clusters

configs.Description: In provider versions prior to 4: The attribute workload_metadata_config.node_metadata configures how node metadata is exposed to workloads. It should be set to SECURE to limit metadata exposure, or GKE_METADATA_SERVER if Workload Identity is enabled. Starting with provider version 4: The attribute node_metadata has been removed. Instead, workload_metadata_configuration.mode controls node metadata exposure. When Workload Identity is enabled, it should be set to GKE_METADATA to prevent unnecessary exposure of the metadata API to workloads.
configs.Message: Cluster exposes node metadata of pools by default.
configs.Resolution: Set mode to GKE_METADATA
configs.Title: Node metadata value disables metadata concealment.

configs.Description: Automatic updates keep nodes updated with the latest cluster master version.
configs.Message: Node pool does not have auto-upgraade enabled.
configs.Resolution: Enable automatic upgrades
configs.Title: Kubernetes should have 'Automatic upgrade' enabled

configs.Description: Enabling private nodes on a cluster ensures the nodes are only available internally as they will only be assigned internal addresses.
configs.Message: Cluster does not have private nodes.
configs.Resolution: Enable private cluster

configs.Description: StackDriver logging provides a useful interface to all of stdout/stderr for each container and should be enabled for moitoring, debugging, etc.
configs.Message: Cluster does not use the logging.googleapis.com/kubernetes StackDriver logging service.
configs.Resolution: Enable StackDriver logging
configs.Title: Stackdriver Logging should be enabled

configs.Description:
You should disable Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC), and instead use Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) in GKE.

RBAC has significant security advantages and is now stable in Kubernetes, so it’s time to disable ABAC.
