configs.Description:
IAM account password policies should ensure that passwords have a minimum length.

The account password policy should be set to enforce minimum password length of at least 14 characters.
configs.Resolution: Enforce longer, more complex passwords in the policy
configs.Title: IAM Password policy should have minimum password length of 14 or more characters.

configs.Description: IAM groups should be protected with multi factor authentication to add safe guards to password compromise.
configs.Resolution: Use terraform-module/enforce-mfa/aws to ensure that MFA is enforced
configs.Title: IAM groups should have MFA enforcement activated.

configs.Description: The root user has unrestricted access to all services and resources in an AWS account. We highly recommend that you avoid using the root user for daily tasks. Minimizing the use of the root user and adopting the principle of least privilege for access management reduce the risk of accidental changes and unintended disclosure of highly privileged credentials.
configs.Resolution: Use lower privileged accounts instead, so only required privileges are available.
configs.Title: The "root" account has unrestricted access to all resources in the AWS account. It is highly recommended that the use of this account be avoided.

configs.Description: CIS recommends that all access keys be associated with the root user be removed. Removing access keys associated with the root user limits vectors that the account can be compromised by. Removing the root user access keys also encourages the creation and use of role-based accounts that are least privileged.
configs.Resolution: Use lower privileged accounts instead, so only required privileges are available.
configs.Title: The root user has complete access to all services and resources in an AWS account. AWS Access Keys provide programmatic access to a given account.

configs.Description: CIS recommends that you apply IAM policies directly to groups and roles but not users. Assigning privileges at the group or role level reduces the complexity of access management as the number of users grow. Reducing access management complexity might in turn reduce opportunity for a principal to inadvertently receive or retain excessive privileges.
configs.Resolution: Grant policies at the group level instead.
configs.Title: IAM policies should not be granted directly to users.

configs.Description: Ensures any IAM pass role attached to roles are flagged and warned.
configs.Resolution: Resolve permission escalations by denying pass role'
configs.Title: IAM Pass Role Filtering

configs.Description: Ensure that the creation of the unrestricted S3 IAM policies is disallowed.
configs.Resolution: Create more restrictive S3 policies
configs.Title: Disallow unrestricted S3 IAM Policies

configs.Description: Unnecessary access to S3 buckets can lead to unauthorized access or information disclosure.
configs.Resolution: Allowing Get* along with Put* on all Resources can potentially allow unauthorized access and/or information disclosure from sensitive buckets.
configs.Title: Reduce unnecessary unauthorized access or information disclosure of S3 buckets.

configs.Description:
Storage container public access should be off. It can be configured for blobs only, containers and blobs or off entirely. The default is off, with no public access.
Explicitly overriding publicAccess to anything other than off should be avoided.
configs.Resolution: Disable public access to storage containers
configs.Title: Storage containers in blob storage mode should not have public access

configs.Description:
You can configure your storage account to accept requests from secure connections only by setting the Secure transfer required property for the storage account.
When you require secure transfer, any requests originating from an insecure connection are rejected.
Microsoft recommends that you always require secure transfer for all of your storage accounts.
configs.Resolution: Only allow secure connection for transferring data into storage accounts
configs.Title: Storage accounts should be configured to only accept transfers that are over secure connections

configs.Description:
Storage Analytics logs detailed information about successful and failed requests to a storage service.
This information can be used to monitor individual requests and to diagnose issues with a storage service.
Requests are logged on a best-effort basis.
configs.Resolution: Enable logging for Queue Services
configs.Title: When using Queue Services for a storage account, logging should be enabled.

configs.Description:
Some Microsoft services that interact with storage accounts operate from networks that can't be granted access through network rules.
To help this type of service work as intended, allow the set of trusted Microsoft services to bypass the network rules
configs.Resolution: Allow Trusted Microsoft Services to bypass
configs.Title: Trusted Microsoft Services should have bypass access to Storage accounts

configs.Description:
Azure Storage currently supports three versions of the TLS protocol: 1.0, 1.1, and 1.2.
Azure Storage uses TLS 1.2 on public HTTPS endpoints, but TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 are still supported for backward compatibility.
This check will warn if the minimum TLS is not set to TLS1_2.
configs.Resolution: Use a more recent TLS/SSL policy for the load balancer
configs.Title: The minimum TLS version for Storage Accounts should be TLS1_2

configs.Description:
The default_action for network rules should come into effect when no other rules are matched.
The default action should be set to Deny.
configs.Resolution: Set network rules to deny
configs.Title: The default action on Storage account network rules should be set to deny

configs.Description: Opening up ports to allow connections from the public internet is generally to be avoided. You should restrict access to IP addresses or ranges that are explicitly required where possible.
configs.Resolution: Set a more restrictive cidr range
configs.Title: A firewall rule should not allow unrestricted ingress from any IP address.

configs.Description: VPC flow logs record information about all traffic, which is a vital tool in reviewing anomalous traffic.
configs.Resolution: Enable VPC flow logs
configs.Title: VPC flow logs should be enabled for all subnetworks

configs.Description: Use of project-wide SSH keys means that a compromise of any one of these key pairs can result in all instances being compromised. It is recommended to use instance-level keys.
configs.Resolution: Disable project-wide SSH keys
configs.Title: Disable project-wide SSH keys for all instances

configs.Description: Instances should not be publicly exposed to the internet
configs.Resolution: Remove public IP
configs.Title: Instances should not have public IP addresses

configs.Description: When serial port access is enabled, the access is not governed by network security rules meaning the port can be exposed publicly.
configs.Resolution: Disable serial port access
configs.Title: Disable serial port connectivity for all instances

configs.Description: Using unmanaged keys makes rotation and general management difficult.
configs.Resolution: Use managed keys
configs.Title: VM disks should be encrypted with Customer Supplied Encryption Keys

configs.Description: Using unmanaged keys makes rotation and general management difficult.
configs.Resolution: Use managed keys to encrypt disks.
configs.Title: Disks should be encrypted with customer managed encryption keys

configs.Description: Opening up ports to connect out to the public internet is generally to be avoided. You should restrict access to IP addresses or ranges that are explicitly required where possible.
configs.Resolution: Set a more restrictive cidr range
configs.Title: A firewall rule should not allow unrestricted egress to any IP address.

configs.Description: OS Login automatically revokes the relevant SSH keys when an IAM user has their access revoked.
configs.Resolution: Enable OS Login at project level and remove instance-level overrides
configs.Title: Instances should not override the project setting for OS Login

configs.Description: Sensitive values such as raw encryption keys should not be included in your Terraform code, and should be stored securely by a secrets manager.
configs.Resolution: Reference a managed key rather than include the key in raw format.
configs.Title: The encryption key used to encrypt a compute disk has been specified in plaintext.

configs.Description: TLS versions prior to 1.2 are outdated and insecure. You should use 1.2 as aminimum version.
configs.Resolution: Enforce a minimum TLS version of 1.2
configs.Title: SSL policies should enforce secure versions of TLS

configs.Description: The virtual TPM provides numerous security measures to your VM.
configs.Resolution: Enable Shielded VM VTPM
configs.Title: Instances should have Shielded VM VTPM enabled

configs.Description: OS Login automatically revokes the relevant SSH keys when an IAM user has their access revoked.
configs.Resolution: Enable OS Login at project level
configs.Title: OS Login should be enabled at project level

configs.Description: Disabling IP forwarding ensures the instance can only receive packets addressed to the instance and can only send packets with a source address of the instance.
configs.Resolution: Disable IP forwarding
configs.Title: Instances should not have IP forwarding enabled

configs.Description: The default service account has full project access. Instances should instead be assigned the minimal access they need.
configs.Resolution: Remove use of default service account
configs.Title: Instances should not use the default service account

configs.Description: Integrity monitoring helps you understand and make decisions about the state of your VM instances.
configs.Resolution: Enable Shielded VM Integrity Monitoring
configs.Title: Instances should have Shielded VM integrity monitoring enabled

configs.Description: Secure boot helps ensure that the system only runs authentic software.
configs.Resolution: Enable Shielded VM secure boot
configs.Title: Instances should have Shielded VM secure boot enabled
